01 // About Me

Data Analyst.
Problem Solver.

I'm Tayyab Kashif, an aspiring Data Analyst and ML practitioner pursuing a BS in Data Analytics at COMSATS University Islamabad. I specialize in transforming complex datasets into insights that actually drive decisions.

My technical work spans deep learning (CNN helmet detection), NLP (multi-model sentiment pipelines), financial investment analysis, big data strategy for global enterprises, and database system design — giving me a versatile, full-stack data perspective.

I've also worked professionally as a Data Analyst Intern at Zarea and as a freelancer on Fiverr, delivering real-world projects in Python, SQL, and machine learning for clients globally.

04 // Projects

Selected Work

⭐ Featured Project · Business / Financial Analysis
Colgate-Palmolive (Pakistan) Investment Pitch Deck

A comprehensive 10-slide investment analysis and pitch deck for Colgate-Palmolive Pakistan Limited, built using real financial data from FY2020–FY2025. The project involved deep analysis of income statements, equity returns, and competitive positioning to make a data-backed investment case.

PKR 156.7B
FY2025 Revenue
27%
Revenue CAGR
49.6%
ROE FY2024
3 vs
Competitors Benchmarked

Key sections covered: company overview, financial KPI analysis (EPS trend, net profit acceleration, ROE recovery), competitor benchmarking vs Glaxo & Haleon Pakistan across 13 metrics (FY2020–FY2024), non-current asset capital deployment, and an investment thesis with 4 clearly argued reasons to invest.

Financial AnalysisInvestment ValuationCompetitive Benchmarking EPS / ROE / CAGRData VisualizationPowerPointFMCG Sector
Project 02 · Deep Learning / Computer Vision
Helmet vs Non-Helmet Detection System

A CNN-based deep learning model deployed as a live web app that classifies whether a rider is wearing a helmet — achieving 100% confidence on helmet detection and 91.7% on no-helmet cases. Built for real-world road safety monitoring.

PythonCNNTensorFlowComputer VisionFlask/Gradio

Project 03 · NLP / Machine Learning
Sentiment Analysis — Multi-Model NLP Pipeline

Built and compared 4 classifiers (BoW + LR, TF-IDF + LR, Averaged Embeddings + LR, SimpleRNN) on a balanced 30K-sample dataset. Stratified 70/15/15 splits, confusion matrices, comprehensive evaluation.
